# admanager_ad_unit (Resource)

Manages a Google Ad Manager [ad unit](https://developers.google.com/ad-manager/api/beta/reference/rest/v1/networks.adUnits). Ad units are nodes in the network's inventory hierarchy.

~> **Destroy archives, it does not delete.** The Ad Manager API has no hard delete for ad units. `terraform destroy` archives the ad unit via `adUnits:batchArchive`. Set `skip_archive_on_destroy = true` to remove the ad unit from Terraform state without touching Ad Manager.

~> **An archived unit keeps its `ad_unit_code` reserved.** Because `parent_ad_unit` and `ad_unit_code` are immutable, changing either forces replacement: Terraform destroys the current unit (archiving it) and creates a new one. The archived unit still holds its `ad_unit_code`, so the replacement create reuses that code and fails with `400 INVALID_ARGUMENT`. To replace a unit that sets `ad_unit_code`, either set `skip_archive_on_destroy = true` on the current unit first (so it stays intact and can be re-adopted with `terraform import`), give the replacement a different `ad_unit_code`, or unarchive and import the existing unit. The provider warns about this during `plan`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# A web ad unit placed directly under the network's root ad unit, serving a
# 300x250 medium rectangle and a 728x90 leaderboard in the browser environment.
#
# The parent must be an existing ad unit's resource name. For a top-level unit,
# use the network's effective root ad unit, available as
# data.admanager_network.current.effective_root_ad_unit (or from the Ad
# Manager UI).

resource "admanager_ad_unit" "homepage" {
  parent_ad_unit = "networks/123456/adUnits/1" # the network's root ad unit
  display_name   = "Homepage"
  description    = "Homepage inventory"

  # Optional, immutable ad serving code. If omitted, Ad Manager assigns one.
  ad_unit_code = "homepage"

  sizes = [
    {
      width            = 300
      height           = 250
      size_type        = "PIXEL"
      environment_type = "BROWSER"
    },
    {
      width            = 728
      height           = 90
      size_type        = "PIXEL"
      environment_type = "BROWSER"
    },
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `display_name` (String) The display name of the ad unit (maximum 255 characters).
- `parent_ad_unit` (String) Resource name of the parent ad unit: `networks/{network_code}/adUnits/{ad_unit_id}`. Use the network's `effectiveRootAdUnit` for top-level units. **Immutable**: changing it forces replacement.

### Optional

- `ad_unit_code` (String) A string that uniquely identifies the ad unit for ad serving. **Immutable**. If omitted, Ad Manager assigns one based on the ad unit ID. Changing it forces replacement.

An `ad_unit_code` stays **reserved by the ad unit that holds it even after that unit is archived**. Because `terraform destroy` archives rather than deletes, replacing a unit that sets `ad_unit_code` (by changing this value or another immutable attribute) archives the old unit and then fails to create the replacement with the same code (`400 INVALID_ARGUMENT`). Use `skip_archive_on_destroy`, a different code, or import-based recovery — see the resource notes above.
- `applied_adsense_enabled` (Boolean) AdSense enablement applied directly to this ad unit. If unset, the value is inherited (see `effective_adsense_enabled`).
- `applied_teams` (List of String) Resource names of Teams applied directly to this ad unit: `networks/{network_code}/teams/{team_id}`.
- `description` (String) A description of the ad unit (maximum 65,535 characters).
- `explicitly_targeted` (Boolean) If `true`, the ad unit is not implicitly targeted when its parent is; line items must target it explicitly. Ad Manager 360 only. Defaults to `false`.
- `external_set_top_box_channel_id` (String) The set-top-box video-on-demand channel this ad unit maps to in an external set-top-box ad system. Deprecated in the API.
- `refresh_delay` (String) The duration after which the ad unit automatically refreshes, as a duration string (e.g. `30s`). Valid only for ad units in mobile apps. If unset, the ad unit does not refresh.
- `sizes` (Attributes List) The sizes that can be served inside this ad unit. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--sizes))
- `skip_archive_on_destroy` (Boolean) When `true`, `terraform destroy` removes the ad unit from state without archiving it in Ad Manager. Provider-side only; never sent to the API. Defaults to `false`.
- `smart_size_mode` (String) The smart size mode: one of `NONE`, `SMART_BANNER`, `DYNAMIC_SIZE`. Defaults to `NONE` for fixed sizes.
- `target_window` (String) The target window applied directly to this ad unit: `TOP` (open in the full page body) or `BLANK` (open in a new window). If unset, the ad unit inherits `effective_target_window`.

### Read-Only

- `ad_unit_id` (String) The numeric ad unit ID, parsed from the resource name for convenience.
- `effective_adsense_enabled` (Boolean) The resolved AdSense enablement, inherited from ancestors when not set directly.
- `effective_target_window` (String) The resolved target window, inherited from ancestor ad units (defaults to `TOP`).
- `has_children` (Boolean) Whether the ad unit has any child ad units.
- `id` (String) The full resource name of the ad unit: `networks/{network_code}/adUnits/{ad_unit_id}`.
- `status` (String) The status of the ad unit: `ACTIVE`, `INACTIVE`, or `ARCHIVED`.
- `teams` (List of String) Resource names of all Teams on this ad unit, including those inherited from ancestors.
- `update_time` (String) The time the ad unit was last modified (RFC 3339).

<a id="nestedatt--sizes"></a>
### Nested Schema for `sizes`

Required:

- `environment_type` (String) The serving environment: `BROWSER` or `VIDEO_PLAYER`. Companions are only valid for `VIDEO_PLAYER`.
- `height` (Number) Height of the size. For non-`PIXEL`/`ASPECT_RATIO` size types this must be `1`.
- `size_type` (String) The size type: one of `PIXEL`, `ASPECT_RATIO`, `INTERSTITIAL`, `IGNORED`, `NATIVE`, `FLUID`, `AUDIO`.
- `width` (Number) Width of the size. For non-`PIXEL`/`ASPECT_RATIO` size types this must be `1`.

Optional:

- `companions` (Attributes List) Companion sizes. Only valid when `environment_type` is `VIDEO_PLAYER`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--sizes--companions))

<a id="nestedatt--sizes--companions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `sizes.companions`

Required:

- `height` (Number) Height of the companion size.
- `size_type` (String) The companion size type: one of `PIXEL`, `ASPECT_RATIO`, `INTERSTITIAL`, `IGNORED`, `NATIVE`, `FLUID`, `AUDIO`.
- `width` (Number) Width of the companion size.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Ad units can be imported by their full resource name.
terraform import admanager_ad_unit.example networks/123456/adUnits/456

# A bare numeric ad unit ID also works; it is expanded against the provider's
# configured network code.
terraform import admanager_ad_unit.example 456
```
